title: BlackBerry shares rise as WSJ report highlights QNX automotive software business momentum
text: BlackBerry shares rose significantly following a Wall Street Journal report highlighting renewed momentum in the company's automotive software business. Shares rose almost 6% on Monday, reaching their highest level in more than a year. BlackBerry has said its QNX technology is now deployed in more than 275 million vehicles worldwide. QNX delivered record Q4 revenue, up 20% year over year, backed by a $950 million royalty backlog and new automotive and defense wins, including Mercedes-Benz, BMW, Volvo, Leapmotor and TKMS. The QNX segment accounted for nearly half of fiscal 2026 revenue, generating $268 million with 20% year-over-year growth in Q4 and 14% for the full fiscal year. The company is leveraging its automotive safety certifications as a blueprint to capture the emerging physical AI and robotics markets. Active discussions are progressing with major OEMs like Mercedes-Benz, with ASP potential being many multiples of the core OS.
